title: The market for loanable funds
description: Practice what you have learned about the market for loanable funds.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the graphs shown below best illustrates the behavior of savers and borrowers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A two-dimensional graph depicting supply and demand curves for a market labeled with variables. The x-axis represents quantity with points marked as Q_LF, while the y-axis represents price with points marked as r_1. A downward-sloping curve labeled D_LF represents demand, and an upward-sloping curve labeled S_LF represents supply. The two curves intersect at a point that indicates equilibrium, represented with a dashed line extending horizontally and vertically to the axes.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac6ceb534e8818f1720506cca087923eb3a85ec1)

![A graph with vertical and horizontal axes labeled "P" for price and "Q" for quantity, respectively. The demand curve is a downward-sloping line labeled "D." There are two supply curves labeled "S1" and "S2," with "S2" positioned above "S1" and both sloping upward. The points of intersection of the demand curve and the supply curves are labeled as "P1" and "P2," corresponding to quantities "Q1" and "Q2," with dashed lines extending vertically to the price axis and horizontally to the quantity axis. The graph includes a grid, but no additional decorations or colors are specified.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58f513d8447e8fc7321ad24a366dd31da3e5abf0)

![A coordinate plane illustrates the supply and demand model, with the vertical axis labeled "Yen per dollar" and the horizontal axis labeled "Quantity in dollars." A green line slopes upwards from left to right, representing the supply curve labeled "S dollar." A red line slopes downwards from left to right, representing the demand curve labeled "D dollar." The two curves intersect at a point labeled "E R one." A dashed vertical line extends from point "E R one" down to the horizontal axis, intersecting it at point "Q one." A dashed horizontal line extends from point "E R one" to the left, intersecting the vertical axis at a coordinate labeled "Q dollar."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fcb0f4f3e176696fc2915f74687248a66c8d319b)

![A downward-sloping demand curve labeled "D sub M" intersects two horizontal dashed lines labeled "i sub 1" and "i sub 2" at marked points. Vertical dashed lines extend from the intersection points to the horizontal axis, which is labeled "Q of $". The graph has an overall structure of an economic demand analysis with axes clearly indicating price and quantity. The dimensions of the graph are marked as 346 units by 348 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fcab26c47e53953151258ad7c7868ab190b24be)

![A graph with two upward-sloping lines and one downward-sloping line. The vertical axis is labeled "P L" with a point marked as "P L 1," and the horizontal axis is labeled "real GDP." The downward-sloping line is labeled "A D," one upward-sloping line is labeled "S R A S," and the other is labeled "L R A S." Two points are marked on the horizontal axis as "Y 1" and "Y f," with a dashed horizontal line connecting "P L 1" to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2c4e0847a080fb46b9fb5bd436887b8f5ab497b8)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would be the appropriate model to use to describe the behavior of borrowers and savers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market for loanable funds
The money market
The foreign exchange market
The AD-AS model
The market model

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how the two axes should be labeled in a model that shows the relationship between borrowers and savers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real interest rate on the vertical axis; quantity of loanable funds on the horizontal axis
Real interest rate on the vertical axis, quantity of money on the horizontal axis
Nominal interest rates on the vertical axis; quantity of money on the horizontal axis
Exchange rate on the vertical axis; quantity of loanable funds on the horizontal axis
Price level on the vertical axis, quantity of loanable funds on the horizontal axis

assessment_items.question_content:
Elements of national income accounting can be abbreviated as shown in the table below.

Abbreviation | Y | C | T | G | I | NX | NCI 
-|-|-|-|-|-|-
Meaning | national income | consumption | taxes | government spending | investment | net exports | net capital inflows
 

**Which of the following equations represents the relationship between savings and investment in a closed economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $Y-C-T=I$
$(Y-C-T) + (T-G) + NCI = I$
$T-G = I$
$NCI=I$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the demand for loanable funds is downward sloping?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: At higher interest rates, the return to investments will be lower
Households prefer to keep some assets in a liquid form so that they can buy things
Higher interest rates discourage spending and encourage savings
At higher interest rates returns to investment will be higher
Higher interest rates discourage savings

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the quantity of loanable funds supplied increases when the real interest rate increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher real interest rate encourages savings
Higher interest rates lower net capital inflows
Higher interest rates encourage investment
Higher interest rates increase the quantity of money supplied
Household consumption increases when interest rates increase

assessment_items.question_content:
Interest rates in Hamsterville have fallen and, as a result, the net return to investment projects are higher than before interest rates fell and now firms are more willing to take out loans.

**Which of the following changes would reflect what is described in the statement above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity of loanable funds demanded increased.
The supply of loanable funds increased.
The demand for loanable funds increased.
The demand for money increased.
The supply of money increased.

assessment_items.question_content:
After interest rates were lowered in Hamsterville, national savings in Hamsterville decreased.


**Which of the following changes would reflect what is described in the statement above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity of loanable funds supplied decreased.
The supply of loanable funds decreased.
The quantity of loanable funds demanded decreased.
The demand for money decreased.
The supply of money decreased.

assessment_items.question_content:
Westley is a citizen of Florin. He believes that the investment conditions in the neighboring country of Guilder are much better.  Assume both countries have an open economy.

**If other investors share Westley’s opinion, what will happen to net capital inflows and the supply of loanable funds in Guilder?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Net capital inflows will increase; the supply of loanable funds will increase
Net capital inflows will decrease; the supply of loanable funds will increase
Net capital flows will not change; the supply of loanable funds will increase
Net capital flows will decrease; the supply of loanable funds will decrease
Net capital inflows will increase; the supply of loanable funds will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is correct about capital inflows?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Capital inflows can be positive, negative, or equal to zero
Capital inflows can only be positive
Capital inflows can only be negative
Capital inflows always equal zero
Capital inflows can be positive or negative, but never equal to zero

assessment_items.question_content:
Elements of national income accounting can be abbreviated as shown in the table below.

Abbreviation | Y | C | T | G | I | NX| NCI 
-|-|-|-|-|-|-
Meaning | national income | consumption | taxes | government spending | investment | net exports | net capital inflows


**Which of the following represents the savings of a country with an open economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $(Y-C-T) + (T-G) + NCI = I$
$Y-C-T=I$
$T-G = I$
$NCI=I$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What relationship does the supply of loanable funds illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Savers respond to an increase in interest rates by saving more money.
Investors respond to an increase in interest rates by investing more money.
Borrowers respond to an increase in interest rates by borrowing more money.
The central bank responds to higher interest rates by supplying more money.
Individuals respond to lower interest rates by increasing the amount of money they hold.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Burginville prohibits the exchange of goods, services, or capital flows between itself and other countries.

**Which of the following best describes national savings in Burginville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Private savings + public savings
Private savings only
Public savings only
Private savings + public savings + net capital inflows
Private savings + public savings + net capital outflows

assessment_items.question_content:
Based on current conditions in the credit market in Patagonia, the amount of money that borrowers want to borrow is greater than the amount of money that savers want to save.

**Which of the following best describes what will happen as a result of this disequilibrium in credit markets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The real interest rate will increase until the quantity of loanable funds demanded equals the quantity of loanable funds supplied
The real interest rate will decrease until the quantity of loanable funds demanded equals the quantity of loanable funds supplied
The supply of loanable funds will increase, lowering the real interest rate
The demand for loanable funds will increase, raising the real interest rate
The supply of money will increase, lowering the real interest rate

assessment_items.question_content:
At the current interest rate of $5\%$, national savings in Florin is currently $\$200$ million but investment demand is currently $\$100$ million.

**Which of the following best describes what will happen once the credit market clears in Florin?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The real interest rate will be lower than $5\%$; the amount of loans made will be between $\$100$ million and $\$200$ million
The real interest rate will be higher than $5\%$; the amount of loans made will be between $\$200$ million and $\$100$ million
The real interest rate will stay $5\%$; the amount of loans made will be $\$200$ million
The real interest rate will increase; the amount of loans made will be $\$200$ million
The real interest rate will decrease; the amount of loans made will be $\$100$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of following best describes national savings in a closed economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Private savings + public savings
Private savings only
Public savings only
Private savings + public savings + net capital inflows
Private savings + net capital inflows

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for loanable funds in Oceania is in equilibrium.

**Based on this statement, which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The amount of borrowing that borrowers want is equal to the amount of savings that savers save.
The interest rate is increasing, attracting more savers into the market.
The interest rate is decreasing, discouraging more borrowers from entering the market.
People want to hold exactly how much money is in the money supply.
Nominal interest equals real interest.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes an equilibrium in the loanable funds market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The real interest rate has adjusted until the quantity of savings supplied is equal to the quantity of borrowing demanded.
The real interest rate has adjusted until the quantity of money supplied is equal to the quantity of money that people want to hold.
The real interest rate has adjusted until public savings and private savings are equal to each other.
The real interest rate has adjusted until more savings are supplied than borrowers want to borrow.
The real interest rate has adjusted until less savings are supplied than borrowers want to borrow.

assessment_items.question_content:
**An equilibrium in the market for loanable funds is most closely associated with which of the following equations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $S=I$
$MV=PY$
$Y=A\times F(K,L)$
$\text{Natural rate of unemployment} = \text{Frictional unemployment } + \text{Structural unemployment}$
$\text{Real interest rate } = \text{Nominal interest rate} - \text{Inflation}$
